items.title: Temporal trends in mitral edge-to-edge repair for primary mitral regurgitation
items.title_zh: PRIME-MR 注册研究:十余年间原发性二尖瓣反流缘对缘修复(M-TEER)疗效与安全性持续提升
items.summary_zh: PRIME-MR 注册研究纳入欧美 27 家中心 3,082 例接受经导管缘对缘修复(M-TEER)治疗的原发性二尖瓣反流(PMR)患者(中位年龄 82 岁,45.1% 为女性),按手术年份分为 2009-2013、2014-2016、2017-2019、2020-2023 四个时期进行比较。结果显示,并发症发生率从 42.4% 降至 23.1%,残余反流≤1+ 的比例从 54.0% 升至 68.4%,术后 1 年无反流复发/加重的“稳定获益”比例从 63.6% 升至 74.4%,提示随着技术与器械迭代,M-TEER 在原发性二尖瓣反流患者中的安全性、即刻疗效与远期结果稳定性均持续改善。
items.summary_en: The PRIME-MR registry analyzed 3,082 patients (median age 82, 45.1% women) undergoing mitral transcatheter edge-to-edge repair (M-TEER) for primary mitral regurgitation across 27 European and North American centers, comparing four procedural eras from 2009-2013 through 2020-2023. Complication rates fell from 42.4% to 23.1%, residual MR ≤1+ rose from 54.0% to 68.4%, and 1-year freedom from MR recurrence or deterioration improved from 63.6% to 74.4% — showing steady gains in procedural safety, efficacy, and durability as M-TEER technique and technology have matured.

items.title: Early outcomes of redo-TAVI with the SAPIEN 3 platform: the prospective, multicentre ReTAVI registry
items.title_zh: ReTAVI 注册研究:SAPIEN 3 平台瓣中瓣再次 TAVI(redo-TAVI)早期结果
items.summary_zh: ReTAVI 是首个专门评估球囊扩张式 SAPIEN 3 平台用于 redo-TAVI(TAVI 瓣膜失功后再次瓣中瓣 TAVI)30 天安全性与有效性的前瞻性国际多中心注册研究,纳入欧洲、加拿大与以色列 59 家中心共 143 例患者(中位年龄 84 岁,40.6% 为女性,中位 STS 评分 7.0%)。原失功瓣膜以 CoreValve/Evolut 系列最常见(53.1%),其次为 SAPIEN 3(30.1%)和 ACURATE(14.0%),为不断增多的 TAVI 术后瓣膜衰败人群的再介入策略提供了首批系统性早期数据。
items.summary_en: ReTAVI is the first prospective, international multicentre study designed specifically to evaluate 30-day safety and efficacy of redo-TAVI using the balloon-expandable SAPIEN 3 platform, enrolling 143 patients (median age 84, 40.6% women, median STS score 7.0%) across 59 centers in Europe, Canada, and Israel. Failed index valves were predominantly CoreValve/Evolut (53.1%), followed by SAPIEN 3 (30.1%) and ACURATE (14.0%) — providing the first systematic early data to guide reintervention strategy as the population of failed TAVI valves grows.

items.title: Alkem Laboratories Completes Acquisition of Majority Stake in Occlutech Holding AG
items.title_zh: 印度制药巨头 Alkem 完成对结构性心脏病器械公司 Occlutech 的控股收购
items.summary_zh: 印度制药企业 Alkem Laboratories 通过全资子公司 Alkem MedTech,完成对瑞士结构性心脏病器械公司 Occlutech Holding AG 51%-55% 控股权的收购,交易股权总价值约 1.807 亿欧元;为筹措资金,Alkem 于 7 月 15 日通过配股向 Alkem MedTech 注入约 11 亿卢比。Occlutech 是欧洲第二、全球第三大微创心脏植入器械厂商,产品覆盖房间隔缺损(ASD)、卵圆孔未闭(PFO)、动脉导管未闭(PDA)封堵器,并有左心耳封堵器在研,此次收购标志着 Alkem 正式进军全球结构性心脏病器械市场。
items.summary_en: Indian pharma major Alkem Laboratories, through wholly owned subsidiary Alkem MedTech, has completed acquisition of a 51-55% controlling stake in Swiss structural heart device maker Occlutech Holding AG, in a deal valued at roughly €180.7 million in total equity; to fund it, Alkem infused about ₹11 billion (~$130M) into Alkem MedTech via a rights issue with shares allotted on July 15. Occlutech, Europe's #2 and the world's #3 player in minimally invasive cardiac occluders, makes ASD, PFO and PDA closure devices and has an LAA occluder in development — marking Alkem's formal entry into the global structural heart device market.

items.title: First-in-human experience with a heterotopic cross-caval transcatheter tricuspid valve replacement
items.title_zh: UNICA 异位腔静脉双瓣系统治疗重度三尖瓣反流:首次人体应用经验
items.summary_zh: 报道 Innoventric 公司研发的 UNICA 系统——一种单支架、双人工瓣膜的异位腔静脉入路装置(经股静脉输送,24 Fr 输送系统,无需全麻或经食道超声),可同时消除右房-下腔静脉与右房-上腔静脉两处反流,区别于此前只处理下腔静脉端的 TRICENTO、Trillium 等异位装置。2023 年 1 月至 2024 年 1 月,欧洲与加拿大 7 家中心在“慈悲使用”(compassionate use)框架下为 8 例重度三尖瓣反流患者植入该系统,首次人体经验提示该新型异位路径具有可行性,可为无法行原位三尖瓣介入的重症患者提供新选择。
items.summary_en: This report describes UNICA (Innoventric), a single-stent, double-valve heterotopic cross-caval device delivered transfemorally under fluoroscopy alone (24 Fr system, no general anesthesia or TEE needed) that eliminates backflow at both the RA-IVC and RA-SVC junctions — a step beyond earlier heterotopic devices like TRICENTO and Trillium, which addressed only the IVC side. Between January 2023 and January 2024, 8 patients with severe tricuspid regurgitation received UNICA under compassionate use across 7 European and Canadian centers, marking a feasible first-in-human experience with this novel heterotopic approach for patients unsuitable for orthotopic tricuspid interventions.
